evals.prompt: We forked CoPaw from agentscope-ai/CoPaw and ship our own release. I want to merge upstream main into our local main but I have never set upstream. Walk me through the exact git commands and what to do if merge conflicts appear in deploy/Dockerfile.
evals.expected_output: Adds upstream remote, fetches, merges upstream/<default-branch>, explains conflict resolution for Dockerfile, suggests running tests after merge

evals.prompt: Upstream PR 42 fixes a bug we need, but it is still open. I only want those commits on our branch `local`, not a full upstream merge. How do I fetch that PR and cherry-pick safely?
evals.expected_output: Uses git fetch upstream pull/42/head, inspects log, cherry-picks onto a topic branch from local, mentions gh pr view as optional

evals.prompt: After cherry-picking three commits from upstream, `git cherry-pick` stopped with conflicts in next-console/package.json. I do not want to lose our pnpm overrides. What should I check before continuing the cherry-pick?
evals.expected_output: Open conflicted file, preserve downstream overrides while taking upstream dependency changes intentionally, git add and cherry-pick --continue, or abort path
